1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	"""Platform backend registry.
3	
4	Each accelerator sub-package (``platform/cuda``, ``platform/cpu``,
5	future ``platform/xpu`` ...) ships a concrete
6	:class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.base_ipc_wrapper.DeviceIPCWrapper`
7	subclass with a ``device_type`` ClassVar and a ``wrap`` factory
8	classmethod.  :func:`_discover_wrappers_once` scans the ``platform``
9	package for those subclasses at run-time and populates the factory
10	table -- no static ``register_kv_wrapper`` calls needed.
11	
12	The :func:`get_kv_wrapper_factory` lookup keys on
13	``tensor.device.type`` so the call site in
14	:mod:`lmcache.integration.vllm.vllm_multi_process_adapter` stays free
15	of any if/elif chain.  Adding a new accelerator therefore requires
16	*zero* changes to the dispatcher; it only needs to ship its own
17	sub-package with a ``DeviceIPCWrapper`` subclass that sets
18	``device_type`` and ``wrap``.
19	"""
20	
21	# Future
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	# Standard
25	from typing import Any, Callable, Dict
26	import threading
27	
28	# First Party
29	from lmcache.logging import init_logger
30	
31	logger = init_logger(__name__)
32	
33	# Public sentinel used by callers who want the always-available
34	# fall-back regardless of the running ``torch_device_type``.
35	DEFAULT_BACKEND: str = "cpu"
36	
37	
38	# KV-cache IPC wrapper factory per device type.  Populated lazily on
39	# first :func:`get_kv_wrapper_factory` call by scanning the
40	# ``platform`` package for
41	# :class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.base_ipc_wrapper.DeviceIPCWrapper`
42	# subclasses.  Tests substitute entries via
43	# :func:`snapshot` / :func:`restore`.
44	_KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES: Dict[str, Callable[..., Any]] = {}
45	
46	# Guard so discovery only runs once (lazy init).  The lock plus the
47	# double-checked flag below keep the first concurrent caller from
48	# racing a second one through the scan and emitting duplicate
49	# "multiple wrappers claim device_type=..." warnings.
50	_WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED: bool = False
51	_DISCOVERY_LOCK = threading.Lock()
52	
53	
54	def _discover_wrappers_once() -> None:
55	    """Populate :data:`_KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES` on first use.
56	
57	    Walks ``lmcache.v1.platform`` two levels deep for
58	    :class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.base_ipc_wrapper.DeviceIPCWrapper`
59	    subclasses.  Each subclass is indexed by its *device_type*
60	    ClassVar, and its *wrap* factory is stored as the KV-wrapper
61	    factory — but only when ``_is_default_wrapper`` is ``True``
62	    (so e.g. :class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.cuda.ipc_wrapper.RawCudaIPCWrapper`
63	    is skipped in favour of
64	    :class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.cuda.ipc_wrapper.CudaIPCWrapper`).
65	
66	    Subclasses with an empty *device_type* or ``_is_default_wrapper ==
67	    False`` are skipped.  Multiple subclasses claiming the same
68	    *device_type* trigger a warning; the first one wins.
69	    """
70	    global _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED
71	    # Fast path: avoid the lock once discovery is done (the common case).
72	    if _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED:
73	        return
74	
75	    with _DISCOVERY_LOCK:
76	        # Re-check under the lock: another thread may have run the
77	        # scan while we were waiting.
78	        if _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED:
79	            return
80	
81	        # First Party
82	        from lmcache.v1.platform.base_ipc_wrapper import DeviceIPCWrapper
83	        from lmcache.v1.utils.subclass_discovery import discover_subclasses
84	        import lmcache.v1.platform as platform_pkg
85	
86	        for cls in discover_subclasses(
87	            platform_pkg,
88	            DeviceIPCWrapper,  # type: ignore[type-abstract]
89	            levels=[2, 2],
90	        ):
91	            _register_discovered_wrapper(cls)
92	
93	        _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED = True
94	
95	
96	def _register_discovered_wrapper(cls: type) -> None:
97	    """Index *cls* in :data:`_KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES` by its device_type.
98	
99	    Only registers when ``_is_default_wrapper`` is ``True`` so sibling
100	    subclasses (e.g. ``RawCudaIPCWrapper`` vs ``CudaIPCWrapper``) can
101	    share a ``device_type`` without colliding.
102	    """
103	    if not getattr(cls, "_is_default_wrapper", False):
104	        return
105	
106	    device_type: str = getattr(cls, "device_type", "")
107	    if not device_type:
108	        logger.warning(
109	            "Skipping %s: empty device_type ClassVar; concrete "
110	            "DeviceIPCWrapper subclasses must override it.",
111	            cls.__name__,
112	        )
113	        return
114	
115	    factory = getattr(cls, "wrap", cls)
116	    existing = _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES.get(device_type)
117	    if existing is not None and existing is not factory:
118	        logger.warning(
119	            "Multiple KV-wrapper classes claim device_type=%r "
120	            "(%s vs %s); keeping the first.",
121	            device_type,
122	            getattr(existing, "__name__", str(existing)),
123	            cls.__name__,
124	        )
125	        return
126	
127	    _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES[device_type] = factory
128	
129	
130	def register_kv_wrapper(device_type: str, factory: Callable[..., Any]) -> None:
131	    """Register a KV-cache IPC wrapper factory for ``device_type``.
132	
133	    This is the manual registration path kept for backward
134	    compatibility.  New backends should instead set ``device_type``
135	    and ``wrap`` on their :class:`DeviceIPCWrapper` subclass and let
136	    :func:`_discover_wrappers_once` handle registration.
137	
138	    Args:
139	        device_type: The device type string (e.g., ``"cuda"``).
140	        factory: A callable that takes a single ``torch.Tensor`` and
141	            returns a wrapper instance ready for the multiprocess wire.
142	    """
143	    _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES[device_type] = factory
144	
145	
146	def get_kv_wrapper_factory(device_type: str) -> Callable[..., Any]:
147	    """Pick the KV-cache wrapper factory for ``device_type``.
148	
149	    Triggers lazy auto-discovery on first call (see
150	    :func:`_discover_wrappers_once`).  A missing entry means no
151	    :class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.base_ipc_wrapper.DeviceIPCWrapper`
152	    subclass declared *device_type* for the requested backend.
153	
154	    Args:
155	        device_type: The device type string (e.g., ``"cuda"``).
156	
157	    Returns:
158	        The registered KV-cache wrapper factory for the device type.
159	
160	    Raises:
161	        ValueError: If no factory is registered for the device type.
162	    """
163	    _discover_wrappers_once()
164	    factory = _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES.get(device_type)
165	    if factory is None:
166	        raise ValueError(
167	            "No KV-cache wrapper factory registered for device type %r" % device_type
168	        )
169	    return factory
170	
171	
172	def snapshot() -> Dict[str, Any]:
173	    """Return a deep-copy of the registry tables.
174	
175	    Test suites use this to install backend overrides without leaking
176	    state across tests; pair with :func:`restore` in a ``finally`` /
177	    fixture teardown clause.
178	
179	    The lazy-discovery flag is captured alongside the tables: if a test
180	    snapshots *before* discovery runs and restores *after*, the next
181	    caller still re-runs discovery and picks up the auto-registered
182	    backends, instead of seeing a stale "already discovered, table is
183	    empty" view.
184	
185	    Returns:
186	        A dict with keys ``"kv_wrapper"``, and ``"discovered"``.
187	    """
188	    return {
189	        "kv_wrapper": dict(_KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES),
190	        "discovered": _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED,
191	    }
192	
193	
194	def restore(state: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
195	    """Restore registry tables to a previously :func:`snapshot`-ed state.
196	
197	    Args:
198	        state: A snapshot dict as returned by :func:`snapshot`.
199	    """
200	    global _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED
201	    _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES.clear()
202	    _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES.update(state.get("kv_wrapper", {}))
203	    _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED = bool(state.get("discovered", False))
204	
205	
206	def reset_for_tests() -> None:
207	    """Wipe registry tables and force re-discovery on next access.
208	
209	    Intended **only** for test fixtures: clears every registered KV
210	    wrapper and flips
211	    :data:`_WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED` back to ``False`` so the next
212	    :func:`get_kv_wrapper_factory` call re-runs the
213	    :func:`_discover_wrappers_once` scan and re-populates the table
214	    from the live ``platform`` sub-packages.
215	
216	    This is the recommended replacement for callers that previously
217	    hand-mutated module-private globals; pair with an ``autouse``
218	    pytest fixture to guarantee every test starts and ends with a
219	    clean slate (see ``tests/v1/multiprocess/conftest.py``).
220	    """
221	    global _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED
222	    _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES.clear()

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""Platform backend registry.

Each accelerator sub-package (``platform/cuda``, ``platform/cpu``,
future ``platform/xpu`` ...) ships a concrete
:class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.base_ipc_wrapper.DeviceIPCWrapper`
subclass with a ``device_type`` ClassVar and a ``wrap`` factory
classmethod.  :func:`_discover_wrappers_once` scans the ``platform``
package for those subclasses at run-time and populates the factory
table -- no static ``register_kv_wrapper`` calls needed.

The :func:`get_kv_wrapper_factory` lookup keys on
``tensor.device.type`` so the call site in
:mod:`lmcache.integration.vllm.vllm_multi_process_adapter` stays free
of any if/elif chain.  Adding a new accelerator therefore requires
*zero* changes to the dispatcher; it only needs to ship its own
sub-package with a ``DeviceIPCWrapper`` subclass that sets
``device_type`` and ``wrap``.
"""

# Future
from __future__ import annotations

# Standard
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict
import threading

# First Party
from lmcache.logging import init_logger

logger = init_logger(__name__)

# Public sentinel used by callers who want the always-available
# fall-back regardless of the running ``torch_device_type``.
DEFAULT_BACKEND: str = "cpu"


# KV-cache IPC wrapper factory per device type.  Populated lazily on
# first :func:`get_kv_wrapper_factory` call by scanning the
# ``platform`` package for
# :class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.base_ipc_wrapper.DeviceIPCWrapper`
# subclasses.  Tests substitute entries via
# :func:`snapshot` / :func:`restore`.
_KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES: Dict[str, Callable[..., Any]] = {}

# Guard so discovery only runs once (lazy init).  The lock plus the
# double-checked flag below keep the first concurrent caller from
# racing a second one through the scan and emitting duplicate
# "multiple wrappers claim device_type=..." warnings.
_WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED: bool = False
_DISCOVERY_LOCK = threading.Lock()


def _discover_wrappers_once() -> None:
    """Populate :data:`_KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES` on first use.

    Walks ``lmcache.v1.platform`` two levels deep for
    :class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.base_ipc_wrapper.DeviceIPCWrapper`
    subclasses.  Each subclass is indexed by its *device_type*
    ClassVar, and its *wrap* factory is stored as the KV-wrapper
    factory — but only when ``_is_default_wrapper`` is ``True``
    (so e.g. :class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.cuda.ipc_wrapper.RawCudaIPCWrapper`
    is skipped in favour of
    :class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.cuda.ipc_wrapper.CudaIPCWrapper`).

    Subclasses with an empty *device_type* or ``_is_default_wrapper ==
    False`` are skipped.  Multiple subclasses claiming the same
    *device_type* trigger a warning; the first one wins.
    """
    global _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED
    # Fast path: avoid the lock once discovery is done (the common case).
    if _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED:
        return

    with _DISCOVERY_LOCK:
        # Re-check under the lock: another thread may have run the
        # scan while we were waiting.
        if _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED:
            return

        # First Party
        from lmcache.v1.platform.base_ipc_wrapper import DeviceIPCWrapper
        from lmcache.v1.utils.subclass_discovery import discover_subclasses
        import lmcache.v1.platform as platform_pkg

        for cls in discover_subclasses(
            platform_pkg,
            DeviceIPCWrapper,  # type: ignore[type-abstract]
            levels=[2, 2],
        ):
            _register_discovered_wrapper(cls)

        _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED = True


def _register_discovered_wrapper(cls: type) -> None:
    """Index *cls* in :data:`_KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES` by its device_type.

    Only registers when ``_is_default_wrapper`` is ``True`` so sibling
    subclasses (e.g. ``RawCudaIPCWrapper`` vs ``CudaIPCWrapper``) can
    share a ``device_type`` without colliding.
    """
    if not getattr(cls, "_is_default_wrapper", False):
        return

    device_type: str = getattr(cls, "device_type", "")
    if not device_type:
        logger.warning(
            "Skipping %s: empty device_type ClassVar; concrete "
            "DeviceIPCWrapper subclasses must override it.",
            cls.__name__,
        )
        return

    factory = getattr(cls, "wrap", cls)
    existing = _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES.get(device_type)
    if existing is not None and existing is not factory:
        logger.warning(
            "Multiple KV-wrapper classes claim device_type=%r "
            "(%s vs %s); keeping the first.",
            device_type,
            getattr(existing, "__name__", str(existing)),
            cls.__name__,
        )
        return

    _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES[device_type] = factory


def register_kv_wrapper(device_type: str, factory: Callable[..., Any]) -> None:
    """Register a KV-cache IPC wrapper factory for ``device_type``.

    This is the manual registration path kept for backward
    compatibility.  New backends should instead set ``device_type``
    and ``wrap`` on their :class:`DeviceIPCWrapper` subclass and let
    :func:`_discover_wrappers_once` handle registration.

    Args:
        device_type: The device type string (e.g., ``"cuda"``).
        factory: A callable that takes a single ``torch.Tensor`` and
            returns a wrapper instance ready for the multiprocess wire.
    """
    _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES[device_type] = factory


def get_kv_wrapper_factory(device_type: str) -> Callable[..., Any]:
    """Pick the KV-cache wrapper factory for ``device_type``.

    Triggers lazy auto-discovery on first call (see
    :func:`_discover_wrappers_once`).  A missing entry means no
    :class:`~lmcache.v1.platform.base_ipc_wrapper.DeviceIPCWrapper`
    subclass declared *device_type* for the requested backend.

    Args:
        device_type: The device type string (e.g., ``"cuda"``).

    Returns:
        The registered KV-cache wrapper factory for the device type.

    Raises:
        ValueError: If no factory is registered for the device type.
    """
    _discover_wrappers_once()
    factory = _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES.get(device_type)
    if factory is None:
        raise ValueError(
            "No KV-cache wrapper factory registered for device type %r" % device_type
        )
    return factory


def snapshot() -> Dict[str, Any]:
    """Return a deep-copy of the registry tables.

    Test suites use this to install backend overrides without leaking
    state across tests; pair with :func:`restore` in a ``finally`` /
    fixture teardown clause.

    The lazy-discovery flag is captured alongside the tables: if a test
    snapshots *before* discovery runs and restores *after*, the next
    caller still re-runs discovery and picks up the auto-registered
    backends, instead of seeing a stale "already discovered, table is
    empty" view.

    Returns:
        A dict with keys ``"kv_wrapper"``, and ``"discovered"``.
    """
    return {
        "kv_wrapper": dict(_KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES),
        "discovered": _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED,
    }


def restore(state: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    """Restore registry tables to a previously :func:`snapshot`-ed state.

    Args:
        state: A snapshot dict as returned by :func:`snapshot`.
    """
    global _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED
    _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES.clear()
    _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES.update(state.get("kv_wrapper", {}))
    _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED = bool(state.get("discovered", False))


def reset_for_tests() -> None:
    """Wipe registry tables and force re-discovery on next access.

    Intended **only** for test fixtures: clears every registered KV
    wrapper and flips
    :data:`_WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED` back to ``False`` so the next
    :func:`get_kv_wrapper_factory` call re-runs the
    :func:`_discover_wrappers_once` scan and re-populates the table
    from the live ``platform`` sub-packages.

    This is the recommended replacement for callers that previously
    hand-mutated module-private globals; pair with an ``autouse``
    pytest fixture to guarantee every test starts and ends with a
    clean slate (see ``tests/v1/multiprocess/conftest.py``).
    """
    global _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED
    _KV_WRAPPER_FACTORIES.clear()
    _WRAPPERS_DISCOVERED = False

2	# Future
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	# Standard
6	from contextlib import nullcontext
7	from typing import Any
8	
9	# First Party
10	from lmcache.logging import init_logger
11	
12	logger = init_logger(__name__)
13	
14	
15	class StubDeviceProperties:
16	    """Stub for torch_dev.get_device_properties() return value."""
17	
18	    def __init__(self) -> None:
19	        self.name = "StubCPU"
20	        self.major = 0
21	        self.minor = 0
22	        self.total_memory = 0
23	        self.multi_processor_count = 0
24	        self.uuid = "stub-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"
25	
26	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
27	        return f"StubDeviceProperties(name={self.name!r})"
28	
29	
30	class StubEvent:
31	    """Stub for a CUDA event, used in CPU-only test environments."""
32	
33	    def __init__(
34	        self,
35	        enable_timing: bool = False,
36	        blocking: bool = False,
37	        interprocess: bool = False,
38	    ) -> None:
39	        self.enable_timing = enable_timing
40	        self.blocking = blocking
41	        self.interprocess = interprocess
42	        self._recorded = False
43	        self._handle = b"stub_ipc_handle"
44	
45	    def record(self, stream: Any = None) -> None:
46	        """Mark this event as recorded on the given stream.
47	
48	        Args:
49	            stream: The stream to record on. If None, uses the
50	                current stream.
51	        """
52	        self._recorded = True
53	
54	    def wait(self, stream: Any = None) -> None:
55	        """Make the given stream wait until this event completes.
56	
57	        Args:
58	            stream: The stream that should wait. If None, uses the
59	                current stream.
60	        """
61	        return None
62	
63	    def query(self) -> bool:
64	        """Check whether the event has completed.
65	
66	        Returns:
67	            True always, since the stub has no real work.
68	        """
69	        return True
70	
71	    def synchronize(self) -> None:
72	        """Block the host until this event completes.
73	
74	        No-op in the stub implementation.
75	        """
76	        return None
77	
78	    def elapsed_time(self, end_event: "StubEvent") -> float:
79	        """Return elapsed time in milliseconds between this event and *end_event*.
80	
81	        Args:
82	            end_event: The ending event to measure against.
83	
84	        Returns:
85	            Elapsed time in milliseconds. Always 0.0 for the stub.
86	        """
87	        return 0.0
88	
89	    def ipc_handle(self) -> bytes:
90	        """Return an IPC handle for cross-process sharing.
91	
92	        Returns:
93	            A bytes object representing the IPC handle.
94	        """
95	        return self._handle
96	
97	    @classmethod
98	    def from_ipc_handle(cls, device: Any, handle: bytes) -> "StubEvent":
99	        """Reconstruct a StubEvent from an IPC handle.
100	
101	        Args:
102	            device: The device to associate with the event.
103	            handle: The IPC handle bytes obtained from
104	                :meth:`ipc_handle`.
105	
106	        Returns:
107	            A new StubEvent with interprocess=True and the given handle.
108	        """
109	        ev = cls(interprocess=True)
110	        ev._handle = handle
111	        return ev
112	
113	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
114	        return f"StubEvent(interprocess={self.interprocess}, recorded={self._recorded})"
115	
116	
117	class StubStream:
118	    """Stub for a CUDA stream, used in CPU-only test environments."""
119	
120	    def __init__(self, device: Any = "cpu", priority: int = 0, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
121	        self.device = device
122	        self.priority = priority
123	        self.cuda_stream = 0
124	        # Mirrors the ``ptr`` attribute exposed by ``cupy.cuda.Stream``
125	        # so callers (e.g. ``mp_observability.event_bus``) that pass a
126	        # raw stream pointer to native recorders accept this stub
127	        # without an isinstance check.
128	        self.ptr = 0
129	
130	    def launch_host_func(self, callback: Any, arg: Any = None) -> None:
131	        """Run ``callback(arg)`` synchronously.
132	
133	        ``cupy.cuda.Stream.launch_host_func`` schedules the callback
134	        on the GPU stream's host-side completion queue; with no real
135	        stream there's nothing to wait for, so we just invoke it
136	        immediately. Exceptions are swallowed to mirror the cupy
137	        contract (callbacks are best-effort and must not propagate).
138	        """
139	        try:
140	            callback(arg)
141	        except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
142	            logger.warning("launch_host_func callback raised: %s", e)
143	
144	    def synchronize(self) -> None:
145	        """Block the host until all kernels on this stream complete.
146	
147	        No-op in the stub implementation.
148	        """
149	        return None
150	
151	    def wait_event(self, event: StubEvent) -> None:
152	        """Make this stream wait until *event* completes.
153	
154	        Args:
155	            event: The event this stream should wait for.
156	        """
157	        return None
158	
159	    def wait_stream(self, stream: "StubStream") -> None:
160	        """Make this stream wait until all kernels on *stream* complete.
161	
162	        Args:
163	            stream: The stream whose pending work must finish before
164	                this stream continues.
165	        """
166	        return None
167	
168	    def record_event(self, event: StubEvent | None = None) -> StubEvent:
169	        """Record an event on this stream and return it.
170	
171	        Args:
172	            event: An existing event to record. If None, a new
173	                StubEvent is created.
174	
175	        Returns:
176	            The recorded StubEvent.
177	        """
178	        event = event or StubEvent()
179	        event.record(self)
180	        return event
181	
182	    def query(self) -> bool:
183	        """Check whether all kernels on this stream have completed.
184	
185	        Returns:
186	            True always, since the stub has no real work.
187	        """
188	        return True
189	
190	    @staticmethod
191	    def priority_range() -> tuple[int, int]:
192	        """Return the range of stream priorities.
193	
194	        Returns:
195	            A tuple of (lowest_priority, highest_priority). Always
196	            (0, 0) for the stub.
197	        """
198	        return (0, 0)
199	
200	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
201	        return f"StubStream(device={self.device}, priority={self.priority})"
202	
203	
204	class StubCPUDevice:
205	    """Stub stand-in for torch_dev in CPU-only test environments."""
206	
207	    def __init__(self, device_type: str = "cpu") -> None:
208	        self._device_type = device_type
209	        self._stream = StubStream(device=device_type)
210	
211	        self.Event = StubEvent
212	        self.Stream = StubStream
213	
214	    def is_available(self) -> bool:
215	        """Check whether the device backend is available.
216	
217	        Returns:
218	            False always, since this is a CPU-only stub.
219	        """
220	        return False
221	
222	    def init(self) -> None:
223	        """Initialize the device backend.
224	
225	        No-op in the stub implementation.
226	        """
227	        return None
228	
229	    def device(self, device: Any = None) -> Any:
230	        """Return a context manager that sets the current device.
231	
232	        Args:
233	            device: The device to select. Ignored in the stub.
234	
235	        Returns:
236	            A no-op context manager.
237	        """
238	        return nullcontext()
239	
240	    def current_stream(self, device: Any = None) -> StubStream:
241	        """Return the current stream for the given device.
242	
243	        Args:
244	            device: The device to query. Ignored in the stub.
245	
246	        Returns:
247	            The current StubStream.
248	        """
249	        return self._stream
250	
251	    def default_stream(self, device: Any = None) -> StubStream:
252	        """Return the default stream for the given device.
253	
254	        Args:
255	            device: The device to query. Ignored in the stub.
256	
257	        Returns:
258	            The default StubStream.
259	        """
260	        return self._stream
261	
262	    def stream(self, stream: StubStream | None = None) -> Any:
263	        """Return a context manager that sets the active stream.
264	
265	        Args:
266	            stream: The stream to activate. If None, uses the
267	                current stream.
268	
269	        Returns:
270	            A context manager yielding the active StubStream.
271	        """
272	        return nullcontext(stream or self._stream)
273	
274	    def synchronize(self, device: Any = None) -> None:
275	        """Wait for all streams on the given device to complete.
276	
277	        Args:
278	            device: The device to synchronize. Ignored in the stub.
279	        """
280	        return None
281	
282	    def set_stream(self, stream: StubStream) -> None:
283	        """Set the current stream.
284	
285	        Args:
286	            stream: The stream to make current.
287	        """
288	        self._stream = stream
289	
290	    def device_count(self) -> int:
291	        """Return the number of available devices.
292	
293	        Returns:
294	            1 always for the stub.
295	        """
296	        return 1
297	
298	    def current_device(self) -> int:
299	        """Return the index of the currently selected device.
300	
301	        Returns:
302	            0 always for the stub.
303	        """
304	        return 0
305	
306	    def set_device(self, device: Any) -> None:
307	        """Select the given device.
308	
309	        Args:
310	            device: The device index or identifier to select.
311	                Ignored in the stub.
312	        """
313	        return None
314	
315	    def get_device_properties(self, device: Any = 0) -> StubDeviceProperties:
316	        """Return device properties for the given device.
317	
318	        Args:
319	            device: The device index or identifier to query.
320	                Ignored in the stub.
321	
322	        Returns:
323	            A StubDeviceProperties instance with default values.
324	        """
325	        return StubDeviceProperties()
326	
327	    def empty_cache(self) -> None:
328	        """Release all unoccupied cached memory.
329	
330	        No-op in the stub implementation.
331	        """
332	        return None
333	
334	    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
335	        raise AttributeError(f"StubCPUDevice does not implement '{name}'")
336	
337	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
338	        return f"StubCPUDevice(device_type={self._device_type})"
339

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# Future
from __future__ import annotations

# Standard
from contextlib import nullcontext
from typing import Any

# First Party
from lmcache.logging import init_logger

logger = init_logger(__name__)


class StubDeviceProperties:
    """Stub for torch_dev.get_device_properties() return value."""

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.name = "StubCPU"
        self.major = 0
        self.minor = 0
        self.total_memory = 0
        self.multi_processor_count = 0
        self.uuid = "stub-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return f"StubDeviceProperties(name={self.name!r})"


class StubEvent:
    """Stub for a CUDA event, used in CPU-only test environments."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        enable_timing: bool = False,
        blocking: bool = False,
        interprocess: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        self.enable_timing = enable_timing
        self.blocking = blocking
        self.interprocess = interprocess
        self._recorded = False
        self._handle = b"stub_ipc_handle"

    def record(self, stream: Any = None) -> None:
        """Mark this event as recorded on the given stream.

        Args:
            stream: The stream to record on. If None, uses the
                current stream.
        """
        self._recorded = True

    def wait(self, stream: Any = None) -> None:
        """Make the given stream wait until this event completes.

        Args:
            stream: The stream that should wait. If None, uses the
                current stream.
        """
        return None

    def query(self) -> bool:
        """Check whether the event has completed.

        Returns:
            True always, since the stub has no real work.
        """
        return True

    def synchronize(self) -> None:
        """Block the host until this event completes.

        No-op in the stub implementation.
        """
        return None

    def elapsed_time(self, end_event: "StubEvent") -> float:
        """Return elapsed time in milliseconds between this event and *end_event*.

        Args:
            end_event: The ending event to measure against.

        Returns:
            Elapsed time in milliseconds. Always 0.0 for the stub.
        """
        return 0.0

    def ipc_handle(self) -> bytes:
        """Return an IPC handle for cross-process sharing.

        Returns:
            A bytes object representing the IPC handle.
        """
        return self._handle

    @classmethod
    def from_ipc_handle(cls, device: Any, handle: bytes) -> "StubEvent":
        """Reconstruct a StubEvent from an IPC handle.

        Args:
            device: The device to associate with the event.
            handle: The IPC handle bytes obtained from
                :meth:`ipc_handle`.

        Returns:
            A new StubEvent with interprocess=True and the given handle.
        """
        ev = cls(interprocess=True)
        ev._handle = handle
        return ev

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return f"StubEvent(interprocess={self.interprocess}, recorded={self._recorded})"


class StubStream:
    """Stub for a CUDA stream, used in CPU-only test environments."""

    def __init__(self, device: Any = "cpu", priority: int = 0, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        self.device = device
        self.priority = priority
        self.cuda_stream = 0
        # Mirrors the ``ptr`` attribute exposed by ``cupy.cuda.Stream``
        # so callers (e.g. ``mp_observability.event_bus``) that pass a
        # raw stream pointer to native recorders accept this stub
        # without an isinstance check.
        self.ptr = 0

    def launch_host_func(self, callback: Any, arg: Any = None) -> None:
        """Run ``callback(arg)`` synchronously.

        ``cupy.cuda.Stream.launch_host_func`` schedules the callback
        on the GPU stream's host-side completion queue; with no real
        stream there's nothing to wait for, so we just invoke it
        immediately. Exceptions are swallowed to mirror the cupy
        contract (callbacks are best-effort and must not propagate).
        """
        try:
            callback(arg)
        except Exception as e:  # noqa: BLE001
            logger.warning("launch_host_func callback raised: %s", e)

    def synchronize(self) -> None:
        """Block the host until all kernels on this stream complete.

        No-op in the stub implementation.
        """
        return None

    def wait_event(self, event: StubEvent) -> None:
        """Make this stream wait until *event* completes.

        Args:
            event: The event this stream should wait for.
        """
        return None

    def wait_stream(self, stream: "StubStream") -> None:
        """Make this stream wait until all kernels on *stream* complete.

        Args:
            stream: The stream whose pending work must finish before
                this stream continues.
        """
        return None

    def record_event(self, event: StubEvent | None = None) -> StubEvent:
        """Record an event on this stream and return it.

        Args:
            event: An existing event to record. If None, a new
                StubEvent is created.

        Returns:
            The recorded StubEvent.
        """
        event = event or StubEvent()
        event.record(self)
        return event

    def query(self) -> bool:
        """Check whether all kernels on this stream have completed.

        Returns:
            True always, since the stub has no real work.
        """
        return True

    @staticmethod
    def priority_range() -> tuple[int, int]:
        """Return the range of stream priorities.

        Returns:
            A tuple of (lowest_priority, highest_priority). Always
            (0, 0) for the stub.
        """
        return (0, 0)

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return f"StubStream(device={self.device}, priority={self.priority})"


class StubCPUDevice:
    """Stub stand-in for torch_dev in CPU-only test environments."""

    def __init__(self, device_type: str = "cpu") -> None:
        self._device_type = device_type
        self._stream = StubStream(device=device_type)

        self.Event = StubEvent
        self.Stream = StubStream

    def is_available(self) -> bool:
        """Check whether the device backend is available.

        Returns:
            False always, since this is a CPU-only stub.
        """
        return False

    def init(self) -> None:
        """Initialize the device backend.

        No-op in the stub implementation.
        """
        return None

    def device(self, device: Any = None) -> Any:
        """Return a context manager that sets the current device.

        Args:
            device: The device to select. Ignored in the stub.

        Returns:
            A no-op context manager.
        """
        return nullcontext()

    def current_stream(self, device: Any = None) -> StubStream:
        """Return the current stream for the given device.

        Args:
            device: The device to query. Ignored in the stub.

        Returns:
            The current StubStream.
        """
        return self._stream

    def default_stream(self, device: Any = None) -> StubStream:
        """Return the default stream for the given device.

        Args:
            device: The device to query. Ignored in the stub.

        Returns:
            The default StubStream.
        """
        return self._stream

    def stream(self, stream: StubStream | None = None) -> Any:
        """Return a context manager that sets the active stream.

        Args:
            stream: The stream to activate. If None, uses the
                current stream.

        Returns:
            A context manager yielding the active StubStream.
        """
        return nullcontext(stream or self._stream)

    def synchronize(self, device: Any = None) -> None:
        """Wait for all streams on the given device to complete.

        Args:
            device: The device to synchronize. Ignored in the stub.
        """
        return None

    def set_stream(self, stream: StubStream) -> None:
        """Set the current stream.

        Args:
            stream: The stream to make current.
        """
        self._stream = stream

    def device_count(self) -> int:
        """Return the number of available devices.

        Returns:
            1 always for the stub.
        """
        return 1

    def current_device(self) -> int:
        """Return the index of the currently selected device.

        Returns:
            0 always for the stub.
        """
        return 0

    def set_device(self, device: Any) -> None:
        """Select the given device.

        Args:
            device: The device index or identifier to select.
                Ignored in the stub.
        """
        return None

    def get_device_properties(self, device: Any = 0) -> StubDeviceProperties:
        """Return device properties for the given device.

        Args:
            device: The device index or identifier to query.
                Ignored in the stub.

        Returns:
            A StubDeviceProperties instance with default values.
        """
        return StubDeviceProperties()

    def empty_cache(self) -> None:
        """Release all unoccupied cached memory.

        No-op in the stub implementation.
        """
        return None

    def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> Any:
        raise AttributeError(f"StubCPUDevice does not implement '{name}'")

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return f"StubCPUDevice(device_type={self._device_type})"

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	"""CUDA-specific platform primitives."""
3	
4	# First Party
5	from lmcache.v1.platform.base_device_spec import DeviceSpec
6	from lmcache.v1.platform.base_pin_memory import PinMemoryBackend
7	from lmcache.v1.platform.cuda.pin_memory import CudaPinMemoryBackend
8	
9	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	# Device detection registry entry
11	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
12	
13	
14	class CudaDeviceSpec(DeviceSpec):
15	    """CUDA device specification for the detection registry."""
16	
17	    @property
18	    def device_type(self) -> str:
19	        return "cuda"
20	
21	    @property
22	    def torch_module_name(self) -> str:
23	        return "cuda"
24	
25	    @property
26	    def ops_module(self) -> str | None:
27	        return "lmcache.c_ops"
28	
29	    @property
30	    def pin_memory_backend(self) -> type[PinMemoryBackend] | None:
31	        return CudaPinMemoryBackend
32	
33	    def is_available(self) -> bool:
34	        """Check CUDA availability without importing lmcache.__init__."""
35	        try:
36	            # Third Party
37	            import torch
38	
39	            return torch.cuda.is_available()
40	        except Exception:
41	            return False

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	# Third Party
3	import torch
4	
5	# First Party
6	from lmcache import torch_dev, torch_device_type
7	from lmcache.utils import EngineType
8	from lmcache.v1.config import LMCacheEngineConfig
9	from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.gpu_connectors import GPUConnectorInterface
10	from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.mock_gpu_connector import MockGPUConnector
11	from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.utils import LayoutHints, need_gpu_interm_buffer
12	from lmcache.v1.metadata import LMCacheMetadata
13	
14	# Boolean config flags whose underlying implementations exist only on a
15	# subset of accelerators. Each entry is ``(attr_name, human_label,
16	# supported_devices)``; the human label is what appears in the error message.
17	_DEVICE_SCOPED_VLLM_BOOL_FEATURES: tuple[tuple[str, str, frozenset[str]], ...] = (
18	    ("enable_blending", "config.enable_blending", frozenset({"cuda", "xpu"})),
19	    ("use_gpu_connector_v3", "config.use_gpu_connector_v3", frozenset({"cuda", "xpu"})),
20	)
21	
22	
23	def _validate_vllm_device_features(config: LMCacheEngineConfig) -> None:
24	    """Reject vLLM configurations that can't run on the active device.
25	
26	    Three classes of misconfiguration are rejected here, all *before* any
27	    device-specific construction so the error surfaces as a plain
28	    ``ValueError`` instead of a deep ``torch.cuda.Stream()`` crash or a
29	    ``RuntimeError`` from ``torch.device('musa:0')`` on a non-MUSA build:
30	
31	    1. Any flag in :data:`_DEVICE_SCOPED_VLLM_BOOL_FEATURES` requested on
32	       a device that has no implementation for that path.
33	    2. ``config.use_layerwise=True`` on HPU — HPU only ships
34	       :class:`VLLMPagedMemHPUConnectorV2`; previously, the dispatch silently
35	       fell through to the CUDA layerwise connector.
36	
37	    Args:
38	        config: The LMCache engine configuration.
39	
40	    Raises:
41	        ValueError: For any of the unsupported combinations above.
42	    """
43	    for attr, label, supported_devices in _DEVICE_SCOPED_VLLM_BOOL_FEATURES:
44	        if torch_device_type not in supported_devices and getattr(config, attr):
45	            supported = "/".join(sorted(supported_devices))
46	            raise ValueError(
47	                f"{label}=True is only supported on {supported}; the active "
48	                f"device is '{torch_device_type}'. Set {attr}=False or run "
49	                "on a supported accelerator build."
50	            )
51	
52	    if torch_device_type == "hpu" and config.use_layerwise:
53	        raise ValueError(
54	            "config.use_layerwise=True is not supported on HPU; LMCache "
55	            "ships no layerwise HPU connector. Set use_layerwise=False or "
56	            "run on a CUDA-capable build."
57	        )
58	
59	
60	def CreateGPUConnector(
61	    config: LMCacheEngineConfig,
62	    metadata: LMCacheMetadata,
63	    engine: EngineType,
64	    layout_hints: LayoutHints | None = None,
65	) -> GPUConnectorInterface:
66	    """
67	    Create a GPU Connector based on the configuration and metadata.
68	
69	    Args:
70	        config: The LMCache engine configuration.
71	        metadata: The LMCache metadata.
72	        engine: The serving engine type (EngineType.VLLM, EngineType.SGLANG,
73	                EngineType.TRTLLM, or EngineType.MOCK).
74	        layout_hints: Optional hints from the serving engine about KV cache
75	            layout (e.g. ``{"kv_layout": "HND"}``).
76	    """
77	    use_gpu = need_gpu_interm_buffer(config)
78	
79	    if engine == EngineType.SGLANG:
80	        if torch_device_type == "musa":
81	            raise ValueError(
82	                "SGLang on MUSA is not supported; only the vLLM MUSA "
83	                "connector is available."
84	            )
85	
86	        num_layer, _, chunk_size, num_kv_head, head_dim = metadata.kv_shape
87	        hidden_dim_size = num_kv_head * head_dim
88	        local_worker_id = metadata.local_worker_id
89	        torch_dev.set_device(local_worker_id)
90	        device = torch.device(f"{torch_device_type}:{local_worker_id}")
91	        kv_dtype = metadata.kv_dtype
92	
93	        if torch_device_type == "xpu":
94	            # First Party
95	            from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.xpu_connectors import (
96	                SGLangLayerwiseXPUConnector,
97	                SGLangXPUConnector,
98	            )
99	
100	            if config.use_layerwise:
101	                return SGLangLayerwiseXPUConnector(
102	                    hidden_dim_size,
103	                    num_layer,
104	                    use_gpu=use_gpu,
105	                    chunk_size=chunk_size,
106	                    dtype=kv_dtype,
107	                    device=device,
108	                )
109	            else:
110	                return SGLangXPUConnector(
111	                    hidden_dim_size,
112	                    num_layer,
113	                    use_gpu=use_gpu,
114	                    chunk_size=chunk_size,
115	                    dtype=kv_dtype,
116	                    device=device,
117	                )
118	        else:  # GPU for SGLang
119	            # First Party
120	            from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.gpu_connectors import (
121	                SGLangGPUConnector,
122	                SGLangLayerwiseGPUConnector,
123	            )
124	
125	            if config.use_layerwise:
126	                return SGLangLayerwiseGPUConnector(
127	                    hidden_dim_size,
128	                    num_layer,
129	                    use_gpu=use_gpu,
130	                    chunk_size=chunk_size,
131	                    dtype=kv_dtype,
132	                    device=device,
133	                )
134	            else:
135	                return SGLangGPUConnector(
136	                    hidden_dim_size,
137	                    num_layer,
138	                    use_gpu=use_gpu,
139	                    chunk_size=chunk_size,
140	                    dtype=kv_dtype,
141	                    device=device,
142	                )
143	    elif engine == EngineType.VLLM:
144	        _validate_vllm_device_features(config)
145	
146	        # First Party
147	        from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.gpu_connectors import (
148	            VLLMBufferLayerwiseGPUConnector,
149	            VLLMPagedMemGPUConnectorV2,
150	            VLLMPagedMemGPUConnectorV3,
151	            VLLMPagedMemLayerwiseGPUConnector,
152	        )
153	
154	        local_worker_id = metadata.local_worker_id
155	        torch_dev.set_device(local_worker_id)
156	        device = torch.device(f"{torch_device_type}:{local_worker_id}")
157	
158	        if torch_device_type == "cuda":
159	            # First Party
160	            from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.gpu_connectors import (
161	                VLLMBufferLayerwiseGPUConnector,
162	                VLLMPagedMemGPUConnectorV2,
163	                VLLMPagedMemGPUConnectorV3,
164	                VLLMPagedMemLayerwiseGPUConnector,
165	            )
166	
167	            if config.use_layerwise:
168	                if config.enable_blending:
169	                    return VLLMBufferLayerwiseGPUConnector.from_metadata(
170	                        metadata, use_gpu, device, layout_hints=layout_hints
171	                    )
172	                else:
173	                    return VLLMPagedMemLayerwiseGPUConnector.from_metadata(
174	                        metadata, use_gpu, device, layout_hints=layout_hints
175	                    )
176	
177	            if config.use_gpu_connector_v3:
178	                return VLLMPagedMemGPUConnectorV3.from_metadata(
179	                    metadata, use_gpu, device, layout_hints=layout_hints
180	                )
181	            else:
182	                return VLLMPagedMemGPUConnectorV2.from_metadata(
183	                    metadata, use_gpu, device, layout_hints=layout_hints
184	                )
185	        elif torch_device_type == "xpu":
186	            # First Party
187	            from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.xpu_connectors import (
188	                VLLMBufferLayerwiseXPUConnector,
189	                VLLMPagedMemLayerwiseXPUConnector,
190	                VLLMPagedMemXPUConnectorV2,
191	                VLLMPagedMemXPUConnectorV3,
192	            )
193	
194	            if config.use_layerwise:
195	                if config.enable_blending:
196	                    return VLLMBufferLayerwiseXPUConnector.from_metadata(
197	                        metadata, use_gpu, device
198	                    )
199	                else:
200	                    return VLLMPagedMemLayerwiseXPUConnector.from_metadata(
201	                        metadata, use_gpu, device
202	                    )
203	
204	            if config.use_gpu_connector_v3:
205	                return VLLMPagedMemXPUConnectorV3.from_metadata(
206	                    metadata, use_gpu, device
207	                )
208	            else:
209	                return VLLMPagedMemXPUConnectorV2.from_metadata(
210	                    metadata, use_gpu, device
211	                )
212	        elif torch_device_type == "musa":
213	            # First Party
214	            from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.musa_connectors import (
215	                VLLMPagedMemLayerwiseMUSAConnector,
216	                VLLMPagedMemMUSAConnectorV2,
217	            )
218	
219	            if config.use_layerwise:
220	                return VLLMPagedMemLayerwiseMUSAConnector.from_metadata(
221	                    metadata, use_musa=use_gpu, device=device
222	                )
223	            return VLLMPagedMemMUSAConnectorV2.from_metadata(metadata, use_gpu, device)
224	        elif torch_device_type == "hpu":
225	            # First Party
226	            from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.hpu_connector import (
227	                VLLMPagedMemHPUConnectorV2,
228	            )
229	
230	            return VLLMPagedMemHPUConnectorV2.from_metadata(metadata, use_gpu, device)
231	        else:
232	            raise RuntimeError(f"No supported {torch_device_type} connector found.")
233	
234	    elif engine == EngineType.TRTLLM:
235	        # First Party
236	        from lmcache.v1.gpu_connector.gpu_connectors import TRTLLMGPUConnector
237	
238	        local_worker_id = metadata.local_worker_id
239	        torch_dev.set_device(local_worker_id)
240	        device = torch.device(f"{torch_device_type}:{local_worker_id}")
241	        return TRTLLMGPUConnector.from_metadata(metadata, device=device)
242	
243	    elif engine == EngineType.MOCK:
244	        kv_shape = metadata.kv_shape
245	        return MockGPUConnector(kv_shape=kv_shape)
246	    else:
247	        raise RuntimeError(f"Unsupported engine: {engine}")
248

27	# Store the tensor objects in memory so that they can be accessed
28	# outside the scope of this file
29	_tensor_registry: dict[int, torch.Tensor] = {}
30	_shm_registry: dict[int, shared_memory.SharedMemory] = {}
31	_buf_registry: dict[int, ctypes.Array] = {}
32	_pinned_ptr_registry: dict[int, int] = {}  # ptr -> size, for cudaHostUnregister

34	# Cached copy library for lmcache_memcpy_async (lazy-initialized)
35	_copy_lib_NOT_LOADED = object()
36	_copy_lib: Optional[ctypes.CDLL] = _copy_lib_NOT_LOADED  # type: ignore
37	
38	
39	def _get_copy_lib() -> Optional[ctypes.CDLL]:
40	    """Lazily load and cache the CUDA/ROCm runtime library, or None for CPU fallback."""
41	    global _copy_lib
42	    if _copy_lib is _copy_lib_NOT_LOADED:
43	        # Try to load GPU runtime libraries in priority order: CUDA first, then ROCm
44	        # TODO: ROCm path to be validated on real device
45	        for name, fallback in [
46	            ("cudart", "libcudart.so"),  # NVIDIA CUDA Runtime
47	            ("amdhip64", "libamdhip64.so"),  # AMD ROCm HIP Runtime
48	        ]:
49	            try:
50	                path = ctypes.util.find_library(name)
51	                if path:
52	                    _copy_lib = ctypes.CDLL(path)
53	                else:
54	                    _copy_lib = ctypes.CDLL(fallback)
55	                break  # Successfully loaded, stop trying
56	            except OSError:
57	                continue  # Current library not available, try next
58	        else:
59	            # All GPU libraries failed to load, fall back to CPU
60	            _copy_lib = None
61	    return _copy_lib

64	def _tensor_from_ptr(
65	    ptr: int,
66	    shape: tuple[int, ...],
67	    dtype: torch.dtype,
68	    device: torch.device | str | None = None,
69	) -> torch.Tensor:
70	    """
71	    Create a tensor view over a raw pointer (zero-copy where possible).
72	
73	    Supports both CPU (pinned or regular) and CUDA device pointers.
74	
75	    Args:
76	        ptr:    Raw memory pointer as int (must be non-zero).
77	        shape:  Desired tensor shape.
78	        dtype:  Desired tensor dtype, must match the memory layout.
79	        device: Where the pointer lives.
80	                - None / "cpu" / torch.device("cpu")  → CPU pointer
81	                - "cuda" / "cuda:N" / torch.device("cuda", N) → CUDA pointer
82	                  If None and ptr looks like a CUDA ptr, pass device explicitly.
83	
84	    Returns:
85	        A tensor that shares memory with the original pointer.
86	        For CPU: always zero-copy via ctypes + torch.frombuffer.
87	        For CUDA: zero-copy via torch._C._construct_storage_from_data_pointer
88	                  (PyTorch >= 2.0) or __cuda_array_interface__, with a
89	                  cudaMemcpy D2D fallback.
90	
91	    Raises:
92	        ValueError: if ptr is 0.
93	
94	    Warning:
95	        The caller is responsible for keeping the underlying memory alive
96	        for the entire lifetime of the returned tensor.
97	    """
98	    if ptr == 0:
99	        raise ValueError("Pointer must be non-zero")
100	
101	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
102	    # Normalise device                                                   #
103	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
104	    if device is None:
105	        device = torch.device("cpu")
106	    elif not isinstance(device, torch.device):
107	        device = torch.device(device)
108	
109	    assert isinstance(device, torch.device)
110	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
111	    # Compute size                                                       #
112	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
113	    numel = 1
114	    for dim in shape:
115	        numel *= int(dim)
116	    element_size = torch.empty((), dtype=dtype).element_size()
117	    total_bytes = numel * element_size
118	
119	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
120	    # CPU path                                                           #
121	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
122	    if device.type == "cpu":
123	        return _tensor_from_cpu_ptr(ptr, shape, dtype, numel, total_bytes)
124	
125	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
126	    # CUDA path                                                          #
127	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
128	    if device.type == "cuda":
129	        return _tensor_from_cuda_ptr(ptr, shape, dtype, device, numel, total_bytes)
130	
131	    raise ValueError(
132	        f"Unsupported device type: {device.type!r}. Expected 'cpu' or 'cuda'."
133	    )
134	
135	
136	# ====================================================================== #
137	#  CPU implementation                                                    #
138	# ====================================================================== #
139	
140	
141	def _tensor_from_cpu_ptr(
142	    ptr: int,
143	    shape: tuple[int, ...],
144	    dtype: torch.dtype,
145	    numel: int,
146	    total_bytes: int,
147	) -> torch.Tensor:
148	    """
149	    Zero-copy CPU tensor from a raw host pointer via ctypes + torch.frombuffer.
150	
151	    """
152	    buffer_type = ctypes.c_uint8 * total_bytes
153	    buf = buffer_type.from_address(ptr)
154	    # torch.frombuffer is zero-copy for contiguous byte buffers on CPU.
155	    return torch.frombuffer(buf, dtype=dtype).view(*shape)
156	
157	
158	# ====================================================================== #
159	#  CUDA implementation                                                   #
160	# ====================================================================== #
161	def _tensor_from_cuda_ptr(
162	    ptr: int,
163	    shape: tuple[int, ...],
164	    dtype: torch.dtype,
165	    device: torch.device,
166	    numel: int,
167	    total_bytes: int,
168	) -> torch.Tensor:
169	    """Zero-copy CUDA tensor from a raw device pointer."""
170	
171	    try:
172	        _DTYPE_TO_TYPESTR = {
173	            torch.float16: "<f2",
174	            torch.float32: "<f4",
175	            torch.float64: "<f8",
176	            torch.int8: "|i1",
177	            torch.int16: "<i2",
178	            torch.int32: "<i4",
179	            torch.int64: "<i8",
180	            torch.uint8: "|u1",
181	            torch.bool: "|b1",
182	        }
183	        is_bf16 = dtype == torch.bfloat16
184	
185	        # Determine the correct typestr, smuggle bfloat16 as int16
186	        typestr = "<i2" if is_bf16 else _DTYPE_TO_TYPESTR.get(dtype, "|u1")
187	
188	        class _CudaArrayWrapper:
189	            def __init__(self, ptr_int: int, shape_tuple: tuple, type_str: str):
190	                self.__cuda_array_interface__ = {
191	                    "data": (ptr_int, False),
192	                    "shape": shape_tuple,
193	                    "typestr": type_str,
194	                    "version": 3,
195	                }
196	
197	        t = torch.as_tensor(_CudaArrayWrapper(ptr, (numel,), typestr), device=device)
198	        if is_bf16:
199	            t = t.view(torch.bfloat16)
200	
201	        return t.view(*shape)
202	    except Exception:
203	        pass
204	
205	    # Strategy 2: cudaMemcpy Device-to-Device (Fallback)
206	    libcudart = _get_copy_lib()
207	    if libcudart is None:
208	        raise RuntimeError("Failed to load libcudart/libamdhip")
209	
210	    cudaMemcpy = libcudart.cudaMemcpy
211	    cudaMemcpy.restype = ctypes.c_int
212	    cudaMemcpy.argtypes = [
213	        ctypes.c_void_p,
214	        ctypes.c_void_p,
215	        ctypes.c_size_t,
216	        ctypes.c_int,
217	    ]
218	    _MEMCPY_D2D = 3
219	
220	    dst = torch.empty(numel, dtype=dtype, device=device)
221	
222	    err = cudaMemcpy(
223	        ctypes.c_void_p(dst.data_ptr()),
224	        ctypes.c_void_p(ptr),
225	        ctypes.c_size_t(total_bytes),
226	        ctypes.c_int(_MEMCPY_D2D),
227	    )
228	    if err != 0:
229	        raise RuntimeError(f"cudaMemcpy D2D failed with error code {err}.")
230	
231	    return dst.view(*shape)
232	
233	
234	def _copy_bytes_with_tensor(dst: int, src: int, num_bytes: int) -> None:
235	    """Copy raw bytes between pointers using torch tensor semantics.
236	
237	    Note: This function only works for CPU-accessible memory. For device
238	    memory (CUDA/XPU), use lmcache_memcpy_async with the appropriate runtime
239	    library or PyTorch's tensor copy operations.
240	    """
241	    if num_bytes <= 0:
242	        return
243	
244	    buffer_type = ctypes.c_uint8 * num_bytes
245	    dst_tensor = torch.frombuffer(buffer_type.from_address(dst), dtype=torch.uint8)
246	    src_tensor = torch.frombuffer(buffer_type.from_address(src), dtype=torch.uint8)
247	    dst_tensor.copy_(src_tensor)

307	class PageBufferShapeDesc:
308	    """Python stand-in for the C++ ``PageBufferShapeDesc`` struct.
309	
310	    Mirrors the pybind ``def_readwrite`` attributes in ``csrc/pybind.cpp``
311	    so non-CUDA code paths can construct and inspect shape descriptors
312	    without the compiled extension.
313	
314	    ``block_stride_elems`` captures the *physical* per-block step in
315	    element units (= ``tensor.stride(0)``). For a tightly-packed paged
316	    buffer it equals ``bs * kv_size * nh * hs`` (non-MLA) /
317	    ``bs * nh * hs`` (MLA); for a vLLM KV pool where a group's row is
318	    padded to the pool's maximum row width (e.g. DeepSeek V4 compressor
319	    / indexer caches), it is strictly larger. Downstream kernels must
320	    use this value instead of recomputing a "tight" stride from the
321	    logical shape, otherwise they'll skip into the next block's padding
322	    region and read/write the wrong slots.
323	    """
324	
325	    __slots__ = (
326	        "kv_size",
327	        "nl",
328	        "nb",
329	        "bs",
330	        "nh",
331	        "hs",
332	        "element_size",
333	        "block_stride_elems",
334	        "dtype",
335	    )
336	
337	    def __init__(self) -> None:
338	        self.kv_size: int = 0
339	        self.nl: int = 0
340	        self.nb: int = 0
341	        self.bs: int = 0
342	        self.nh: int = 0
343	        self.hs: int = 0
344	        self.element_size: int = 0
345	        # 0 means "unset — fall back to tight stride"; any downstream
346	        # consumer that needs exact addressing must check this.
347	        self.block_stride_elems: int = 0
348	        self.dtype: torch.dtype | None = None
349

384	def set_shape_desc_dtype(shape_desc: Any, dtype: torch.dtype) -> None:
385	    """Best-effort ``shape_desc.dtype = dtype``.
386	
387	    The pure-Python ``PageBufferShapeDesc`` exposes a ``dtype`` slot so
388	    the CPU fallback kernel can disambiguate float16 vs bfloat16 (both
389	    have ``element_size == 2``). The pybind C++ struct in
390	    ``csrc/pybind.cpp`` has no such field; assignment raises
391	    ``AttributeError`` and is silently swallowed here so call sites
392	    don't need to branch on the active backend.
393	
394	    Args:
395	        shape_desc: A ``PageBufferShapeDesc`` instance (either the
396	            pure-Python fallback or the C++ pybind struct).
397	        dtype: The torch dtype to assign.
398	    """
399	    try:
400	        shape_desc.dtype = dtype
401	    except AttributeError:
402	        pass
403	
404	
405	# Cuda path goes through func cudaHostAlloc, which is
406	# already page aligned by CUDA spec. This fallback shim mirrors that
407	# guarantee so consumers that require page-aligned host buffers, in
408	# particular the Rust raw-block backend when O_DIRECT is enabled, which
409	# requires page-aligned buffer pointer
410	try:
411	    _PAGE_SIZE = os.sysconf("SC_PAGESIZE")
412	except (AttributeError, ValueError, OSError):
413	    _PAGE_SIZE = 4096
414	
415	logger = init_logger(__name__)
416	
417	# Cached one-shot decision: pin host buffers only when an accelerator is
418	# present. Probed lazily on first allocation; if a pinned allocation ever
419	# fails at runtime we flip this to False permanently and fall back to
420	# pageable memory for all subsequent allocations.
421	_use_pinned: Optional[bool] = None
422	
423	
424	def _alloc_page_aligned_pinned_view(size: int) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, int]:
425	    """
426	    Allocate a pinned CPU buffer whose first usable byte is page-aligned,
427	    and return a torch view of ``size`` bytes plus its base pointer.
428	
429	    Internally over-allocates one extra page on a backing tensor, then
430	    slices the aligned region out. The slice shares storage with the
431	    backing tensor, so keeping the slice alive keeps the underlying
432	    memory alive (no need to track the backing tensor separately).
433	    """
434	    # Pin the host buffer when an accelerator is present (probed once).
435	    # StubCPUDevice.is_available returns False on CPU-only hosts.
436	    global _use_pinned
437	    if _use_pinned is None:
438	        _use_pinned = torch_dev.is_available()
439	    try:
440	        backing = torch.empty(
441	            size + _PAGE_SIZE, dtype=torch.uint8, pin_memory=_use_pinned
442	        )
443	    except RuntimeError:
444	        if not _use_pinned:
445	            # Pure host allocation failed (e.g. OOM); nothing to fall back to.
446	            raise
447	        logger.warning(
448	            "Pinned host allocation failed on device '%s'; falling back to "
449	            "unpinned allocation from now on.",
450	            torch_device_type,
451	        )
452	        _use_pinned = False
453	        backing = torch.empty(size + _PAGE_SIZE, dtype=torch.uint8, pin_memory=False)
454	    # First-touch initialization on the entire backing region
455	    backing.fill_(0)
456	    base = backing.data_ptr()
457	    # Distance from `base` to the next page boundary (0..PAGE_SIZE-1).
458	    offset = (-base) % _PAGE_SIZE
459	    aligned_view = backing[offset : offset + size]
460	    return aligned_view, aligned_view.data_ptr()
461	
462	
463	def alloc_pinned_numa_ptr(size: int, numa_id: int = 0) -> int:
464	    """Non-CUDA equivalent of allocating pinned memory with NUMA awareness.
465	    On XPU, uses pin_memory=True (SYCL USM host allocation) for fast transfers.
466	    Note: NUMA node selection is not supported on non-CUDA."""
467	
468	    view, aligned_ptr = _alloc_page_aligned_pinned_view(size)
469	    # view shares storage with its over-allocated backing tensor;
470	    # holding the view in the registry transitively keeps the underlying
471	    # memory alive.
472	    _tensor_registry[aligned_ptr] = view
473	    return aligned_ptr
474	
475	
476	def free_pinned_numa_ptr(ptr: int, size: int | None = None) -> None:
477	    """Non-CUDA equivalent of freeing a previously allocated NUMA pointer."""
478	
479	    # Release the tensor object for that pointer reference
480	    _tensor_registry.pop(ptr, None)

483	def alloc_pinned_ptr(size: int, device_id: int = 0) -> int:
484	    """Non-CUDA equivalent of allocating pinned memory and returning pointer
485	    to it. On XPU, uses pin_memory=True (SYCL USM host allocation) for
486	    fast DMA transfers. On other non-CUDA platforms, pinning is not supported."""
487	
488	    view, aligned_ptr = _alloc_page_aligned_pinned_view(size)
489	    _tensor_registry[aligned_ptr] = view
490	    return aligned_ptr
491	
492	
493	def free_pinned_ptr(ptr: int) -> None:
494	    """Non-CUDA equivalent of freeing a previously allocated pinned pointer."""
495	
496	    # Release the tensor object for that pointer reference
497	    _tensor_registry.pop(ptr, None)

500	def batched_memcpy(src_ptrs: list[int], dst_ptrs: list[int], sizes: list[int]) -> None:
501	    """Non-CUDA equivalent of the native batched memcpy helper."""
502	
503	    if len(src_ptrs) != len(dst_ptrs) or len(src_ptrs) != len(sizes):
504	        raise ValueError(
505	            "batched_memcpy expects equally sized src_ptrs, dst_ptrs, and sizes"
506	        )
507	
508	    for src_ptr, dst_ptr, size in zip(src_ptrs, dst_ptrs, sizes, strict=True):
509	        if size <= 0:
510	            continue
511	        ctypes.memmove(
512	            ctypes.c_void_p(dst_ptr),
513	            ctypes.c_void_p(src_ptr),
514	            size,
515	        )
516

518	def alloc_shm_pinned_ptr(size: int, shm_name: str = "") -> int:
519	    """Non-CUDA equivalent of allocating shared memory pinned pointer.
520	    Uses multiprocessing.shared_memory for cross-platform POSIX shm.
521	    Attempts to pin the buffer via cudaHostRegister for async D2H;
522	    if pinning fails, continues without pinning."""
523	
524	    # Strip leading '/' for SharedMemory name
525	    name = shm_name.lstrip("/") if shm_name else None
526	
527	    # Clean up stale shm segment if it exists
528	    if name:
529	        try:
530	            stale = shared_memory.SharedMemory(name=name, create=False)
531	            stale.close()
532	            stale.unlink()
533	        except FileNotFoundError:
534	            pass
535	
536	    shm = shared_memory.SharedMemory(name=name, create=True, size=size)
537	
538	    array_type = ctypes.c_uint8 * size
539	    buf = array_type.from_buffer(shm.buf)
540	    ptr = ctypes.addressof(buf)
541	
542	    # Store references to keep them alive
543	    tensor = torch.frombuffer(buf, dtype=torch.uint8)
544	    _tensor_registry[ptr] = tensor
545	    _buf_registry[ptr] = buf
546	    _shm_registry[ptr] = shm
547	
548	    # Try to pin the SHM buffer for async D2H copies
549	    if current_device_spec.pin_memory(ptr, size):
550	        _pinned_ptr_registry[ptr] = size
551	
552	    return ptr
553	
554	
555	def free_shm_pinned_ptr(ptr: int, size: int = 0, shm_name: str = "") -> None:
556	    """Non-CUDA equivalent of freeing a shared memory
557	    pinned pointer. Unregisters pinned memory if it was pinned."""
558	
559	    # Unpin if previously registered
560	    if ptr in _pinned_ptr_registry:
561	        current_device_spec.unpin_memory(ptr)
562	        _pinned_ptr_registry.pop(ptr, None)
563	
564	    # Release in order: tensor -> ctypes buf -> shm
565	    _tensor_registry.pop(ptr, None)
566	    _buf_registry.pop(ptr, None)
567	    shm = _shm_registry.pop(ptr, None)
568	    if shm is not None:
569	        shm.close()
570	        shm.unlink()
571

580	        "Hugepages requested but not available on non-CUDA platforms; "
581	        "falling back to regular allocation.",

584	    )

609	def alloc_numa_ptr(size: int, numa_id: int = 0) -> int:
610	    """Non-CUDA equivalent of allocating numa memory and returning pointer
611	    to it. Note: Numa memory is not supported on non-CUDA."""
612	    return alloc_pinned_numa_ptr(size, numa_id)
613	
614	
615	def free_numa_ptr(ptr: int, size: int | None = None) -> None:
616	    """Non-CUDA equivalent of freeing a previously allocated NUMA pointer."""
617	    return free_pinned_numa_ptr(ptr, size)

620	def multi_layer_kv_transfer(
621	    key_value: torch.Tensor,
622	    key_value_ptrs: torch.Tensor | list[torch.Tensor],
623	    slot_mapping: torch.Tensor,
624	    paged_memory_device: torch.device,
625	    page_buffer_size: int,
626	    direction: TransferDirection,
627	    engine_kv_format: EngineKVFormat,
628	    block_size: int = 0,
629	    head_size: int = 0,
630	    skip_prefix_n_tokens: int = 0,
631	):
632	    """
633	    Fully vectorized Python fallback for multi_layer_kv_transfer.
634	    Eliminates ALL token- and KV-level Python loops.
635	    """
636	    if not isinstance(key_value_ptrs, (torch.Tensor, list)):
637	        raise TypeError(
638	            f"Expected torch.Tensor or list, but got {type(key_value_ptrs).__name__}"
639	        )
640	
641	    # TODO: Implement head_size support for HND layouts (NL_X_TWO_NB_NH_BS_HS,
642	    # NL_X_NB_TWO_NH_BS_HS) as next step.
643	    if int(engine_kv_format) in (
644	        int(EngineKVFormat.NL_X_TWO_NB_NH_BS_HS),
645	        int(EngineKVFormat.NL_X_NB_TWO_NH_BS_HS),
646	    ):
647	        raise NotImplementedError(
648	            "HND layouts (NL_X_TWO_NB_NH_BS_HS, NL_X_NB_TWO_NH_BS_HS) "
649	            "are not supported in the non-CUDA fallback. "
650	            "head_size parameter is required but not implemented in this path."
651	        )
652	
653	    # 1. Filter out invalid slots.
654	    #    valid_mask_kv:  on key_value.device, used to index key_value
655	    #    valid_slots:    on paged_memory_device, used to index paged_tensor
656	    kv_device = key_value.device
657	    slots_kv = slot_mapping.to(dtype=torch.long).to(kv_device)
658	    valid_mask_kv = slots_kv >= 0
659	    # Skip the first skip_prefix_n_tokens tokens from transfer.
660	    # This matches the CUDA kernel semantics where the grid starts at
661	    # token_id=0 but indexes key_value/slot_mapping at
662	    # kv_token_id = token_id + skip_prefix_n_tokens.
663	    # By masking them as invalid, the vectorized indexing via valid_mask_kv
664	    # naturally skips them while keeping key_value indices aligned.
665	    if skip_prefix_n_tokens > 0:
666	        valid_mask_kv[:skip_prefix_n_tokens] = False
667	    if not valid_mask_kv.any():
668	        return
669	
670	    valid_slots = slots_kv[valid_mask_kv].to(paged_memory_device)
671	
672	    # 2. Determine architecture variant and tensor dimensions.
673	    is_mla = int(engine_kv_format) in (
674	        int(EngineKVFormat.NL_X_NB_BS_HS),
675	        int(EngineKVFormat.NL_X_NBBS_ONE_HS),
676	    )
677	    is_flash_infer = int(engine_kv_format) == int(EngineKVFormat.NL_X_NB_TWO_BS_NH_HS)
678	
679	    num_layers = key_value.size(1)
680	    hidden_size = key_value.size(3)
681	
682	    # For the flash_infer interleaved layout, pre-compute block-level indices.
683	    if is_flash_infer:
684	        block_indices = valid_slots // block_size
685	        block_offsets = valid_slots % block_size
686	
687	    # Determine the physical shape of the underlying paged tensor
688	    # (used when wrapping a raw pointer).
689	    layer_shape: Tuple[int, ...]
690	
691	    if is_mla:
692	        layer_shape = (page_buffer_size, hidden_size)
693	    elif is_flash_infer:
694	        num_blocks = page_buffer_size // block_size
695	        layer_shape = (num_blocks, 2, block_size, hidden_size)
696	    else:
697	        layer_shape = (2, page_buffer_size, hidden_size)
698	
699	    # 3. Iterate over layers — the only remaining Python-level loop.
700	    for layer_id in range(num_layers):
701	        # --- A. Obtain the physical device-memory view for this layer. ---
702	        if isinstance(key_value_ptrs, list):
703	            paged_tensor = key_value_ptrs[layer_id]
704	        else:
705	            ptr = int(key_value_ptrs[layer_id].item())
706	            # Convert a raw device pointer into a PyTorch tensor view.
707	            paged_tensor = _tensor_from_ptr(
708	                ptr, layer_shape, key_value.dtype, paged_memory_device
709	            )
710	
711	        # --- B. Vectorized bulk data transfer. ---
712	        if is_mla:
713	            # Paged layout : [page_buffer_size, hidden_size]
714	            # key_value layout: [1, num_layers, num_tokens, hidden_size]
715	            if int(direction) == int(TransferDirection.H2D):
716	                lmc_valid = key_value[0, layer_id, valid_mask_kv, :]
717	                paged_tensor.index_copy_(
718	                    0, valid_slots, lmc_valid.to(paged_tensor.device)
719	                )
720	            else:
721	                gathered = paged_tensor.index_select(0, valid_slots)
722	                key_value[0, layer_id, valid_mask_kv, :] = gathered.to(
723	                    kv_device, non_blocking=False
724	                )
725	        elif is_flash_infer:
726	            # Paged layout : [num_blocks, 2, block_size, hidden_size]
727	            # key_value layout: [2, num_layers, num_tokens, hidden_size]
728	            if int(direction) == int(TransferDirection.H2D):
729	                lmc_valid = key_value[:, layer_id, valid_mask_kv, :]
730	                src_data = lmc_valid.transpose(0, 1).to(paged_memory_device)
731	                # src_data: [num_valid, 2, hidden_size]
732	                paged_tensor[block_indices, :, block_offsets, :] = src_data
733	            else:
734	                gathered = paged_tensor[block_indices, :, block_offsets, :]
735	                # gathered: [num_valid, 2, hidden_size]
736	                key_value[:, layer_id, valid_mask_kv, :] = gathered.to(
737	                    kv_device, non_blocking=False
738	                ).transpose(0, 1)
739	        else:
740	            # Paged layout : [2, page_buffer_size, hidden_size]
741	            # key_value layout: [2, num_layers, num_tokens, hidden_size]
742	            if int(direction) == int(TransferDirection.H2D):
743	                lmc_valid = key_value[:, layer_id, valid_mask_kv, :]
744	                paged_tensor.index_copy_(
745	                    1, valid_slots, lmc_valid.to(paged_memory_device)
746	                )
747	            else:
748	                gathered = paged_tensor.index_select(1, valid_slots)
749	                key_value[:, layer_id, valid_mask_kv, :] = gathered.to(
750	                    kv_device, non_blocking=False
751	                )
752	
753	
754	def multi_layer_kv_transfer_unilateral(
755	    key_value: torch.Tensor,
756	    key_value_ptrs: torch.Tensor | list[torch.Tensor],
757	    slot_mapping: torch.Tensor,
758	    paged_memory_device: torch.device,
759	    page_buffer_size: int,
760	    direction: TransferDirection,
761	    engine_kv_format: EngineKVFormat,
762	):
763	    """
764	    Python fallback for multi_layer_kv_transfer_unilateral
765	
766	    Handles SGLang MHA format where K and V paged buffers are stored separately:
767	        ptrs = [K_layer0, K_layer1, ..., V_layer0, V_layer1, ...]
768	        each buffer shape: [page_buffer_size, hidden_size]
769	
770	    For MLA, delegates to multi_layer_kv_transfer (same as C++ implementation).
771	
772	    key_value_ptrs:
773	        - If torch.Tensor: int64 tensor containing raw memory pointers.
774	        - If list[torch.Tensor]: list of tensor objects.
775	
776	    key_value layout:
777	        - Standard: [2, num_layers, num_tokens, hidden_size]
778	        - MLA:      [1, num_layers, num_tokens, hidden_size]
779	
780	    direction:
781	        H2D = LMCache  -> PagedBuffer
782	        D2H = PagedBuffer -> LMCache
783	    """
784	    is_mla = int(engine_kv_format) in (
785	        int(EngineKVFormat.NL_X_NB_BS_HS),
786	        int(EngineKVFormat.NL_X_NBBS_ONE_HS),
787	    )
788	
789	    # MLA case collapses back to multi_layer_kv_transfer
790	    # (vLLM and SGLang indexing are compatible)
791	    if is_mla:
792	        return multi_layer_kv_transfer(
793	            key_value,
794	            key_value_ptrs,
795	            slot_mapping,
796	            paged_memory_device,
797	            page_buffer_size,
798	            direction,
799	            engine_kv_format,
800	            0,  # block_size unused for MLA formats
801	        )
802	    # ── Non-MLA path: unilateral (separate K/V buffers per layer) ──
803	    num_layers = key_value.size(1)
804	    hidden_size = key_value.size(3)
805	    layer_shape = (page_buffer_size, hidden_size)
806	
807	    kv_device = key_value.device
808	    slots_kv = slot_mapping.to(dtype=torch.long).to(kv_device)
809	    valid_mask_kv = slots_kv >= 0
810	    if not valid_mask_kv.any():
811	        return
812	
813	    valid_slots = slots_kv[valid_mask_kv].to(paged_memory_device)
814	
815	    for layer_id in range(num_layers):
816	        for kv_idx in range(2):  # 0 = K, 1 = V
817	            buffer_idx = layer_id + kv_idx * num_layers
818	            if isinstance(key_value_ptrs, list):
819	                paged_tensor = key_value_ptrs[buffer_idx]
820	            else:
821	                ptr = int(key_value_ptrs[buffer_idx].item())
822	                paged_tensor = _tensor_from_ptr(
823	                    ptr, layer_shape, key_value.dtype, paged_memory_device
824	                )
825	
826	            if int(direction) == int(TransferDirection.H2D):
827	                lmc_valid = key_value[kv_idx, layer_id, valid_mask_kv, :]
828	                paged_tensor.index_copy_(
829	                    0, valid_slots, lmc_valid.to(paged_memory_device)
830	                )
831	            else:
832	                gathered = paged_tensor.index_select(0, valid_slots)
833	                key_value[kv_idx, layer_id, valid_mask_kv, :] = gathered.to(kv_device)
834	
835

837	# parity test pins these to the same names and signatures.

841	        int(EngineKVFormat.NB_NL_TWO_BS_NH_HS),
842	        int(EngineKVFormat.NB_NL_TWO_NH_BS_HS),
843	    )

847	    """Return True when keys and values are two separate top-level lists."""

849	        int(EngineKVFormat.TWO_X_NL_X_NBBS_NH_HS),
850	        int(EngineKVFormat.TWO_X_NL_X_NB_BS_NH_HS),

860	        int(EngineKVFormat.NL_X_NBBS_ONE_HS),
861	        int(EngineKVFormat.NL_X_TWO_NB_NH_BS_HS),
862	        int(EngineKVFormat.NL_X_NB_TWO_NH_BS_HS),
